summary: A payment submission form web app where customers can fill in their details including Name, Address, Email, 16-digit Card Number, Expiry Date, CVV, and Amount. The app features a clean light theme (white/off-white background, amber/gold accent).
known_issues: Admin panel fails to load payments after login — shows 'Failed to load payments, please try refreshing' error
Payment submission fails with 'Payment submission failed. Please try again.' error
Payment form shows 'Payment service not ready. Please wait a moment and try again.' error on submission
Admin panel does not display submitted payment records after submission

existing_features.title: Build a payment submission form page with input fields for: Full Name, Address, Email, 16-digit Card Number (auto-formatted with spaces every 4 digits), Expiry Date (MM/YY), CVV, and Amount. Validate all fields before submission. Show real-time inline validation errors. On successful submission, show a confirmation message.

Apply a clean, professional financial/fintech visual theme with a dark navy and gold color palette, modern card-style form layout, subtle shadows, and elegant typography. The form should feel secure and trustworthy. Enforce the theme consistently across the entire UI.
Generate a payment form hero/background image and a secure payment lock icon to enhance the visual design of the form.

Replace the current dark navy and gold color palette with a clean light theme. Use a white or off-white background, light card surfaces, and a refined accent color (e.g., a muted gold or warm amber on light) for interactive elements like buttons, focus rings, and highlights. Ensure all text has sufficient contrast on light backgrounds. Update all custom CSS properties, Tailwind theme tokens, and component classes (`.payment-card`, `.payment-input`, `.btn-gold`, etc.) to reflect the new light palette. Apply the light theme consistently across the entire UI: header, form card, input fields, validation errors, success confirmation screen, trust badges, and footer.

Add an AdminPage component that fetches all submitted payment records from the backend `getAllPayments` query endpoint and displays them in a styled table. Each row should show: Full Name, Email, Address, Card Number (masked, showing only the last 4 digits), Expiry Date, and Amount. Apply the existing light theme (white/off-white card surface, amber/gold accent, neutral typography) consistently to the admin view.
Add a React Query query hook in `useQueries.ts` that calls the backend `getAllPayments` actor method and returns the list of payment records for use in the AdminPage.

Update `App.tsx` to support routing between the PaymentPage and AdminPage. Add an 'Admin' navigation link in the sticky header on `PaymentPage` that navigates to the admin view. Ensure the existing PaymentPage remains the default route.

Protect the AdminPage route so that only authenticated users (logged in via Internet Identity) can access it. Unauthenticated visitors who navigate to the admin view must be shown a login prompt instead of the admin table.

Add a styled login gate component rendered within the AdminPage area when the user is not authenticated. The component should display a lock icon or secure badge, a short message explaining that admin access is restricted, and a 'Login with Internet Identity' button styled consistently with the existing light amber/neutral theme.
Display the currently authenticated user's Internet Identity principal (or a truncated version of it) in the AdminPage header alongside the Logout button, so admins can confirm their identity.

Fix the AdminPage so that after a successful Internet Identity login, the full payments table is rendered and populated with all payment records. Ensure the authentication state is correctly detected post-login (i.e., the component re-evaluates the `isAuthenticated` flag after the login flow completes and the actor is ready), and that `useGetAllPayments` is triggered and its data is displayed in the table rather than the login gate or an empty/loading state.

Ensure the `useGetAllPayments` React Query hook in `useQueries.ts` correctly uses the authenticated actor (not the anonymous actor) after login, so that the backend `getAllPayments` query executes successfully and returns data once the user is authenticated. Invalidate or refetch the query when the actor/identity changes.

Diagnose and fix the root cause of the 'Failed to load payments — Please try refreshing the page' error shown in AdminPage. Investigate the `useGetAllPayments` hook in `useQueries.ts` and the `getAllPayments` call in `AdminPage.tsx`. Ensure the query does not execute until the authenticated actor is fully ready (i.e., the actor instance is non-null and reflects the logged-in identity). Add robust error handling so that transient actor initialization errors are retried automatically (e.g., via React Query's `retry` option) rather than immediately surfacing an error state to the user.

Ensure the `useActor` hook in the frontend correctly signals actor readiness after Internet Identity login completes. The actor instance exposed to consumers must only be considered 'ready' once the underlying `AuthClient` has fully resolved and the identity has been applied. Any dependent React Query hooks (such as `useGetAllPayments`) must re-evaluate their enabled state and re-fetch when the actor transitions from anonymous/uninitialized to authenticated.

In `AdminPage.tsx`, replace the current immediate error display on `useGetAllPayments` failure with a loading/retry state during the actor initialization window. Show the 'Failed to load payments' error message only after all automatic retries are exhausted and the actor is confirmed to be in an authenticated, ready state.

Diagnose and fix the 'Payment submission failed. Please try again.' error in the payment form. Inspect the `useSubmitPayment` mutation hook in `useQueries.ts` and the `submitPayment` call in `PaymentForm.tsx`. Ensure the anonymous actor is correctly initialised before the mutation fires, that the canister method signature matches the frontend call arguments, and that any rejection from the backend is surfaced with a descriptive error message rather than a generic failure string. Add retry logic (at least 2 retries) for transient network errors.

Apply a POS machine / credit card terminal visual theme to the entire payment UI. The design should evoke a physical point-of-sale terminal: a dark charcoal or gunmetal device body background, a raised card-slot or card-reader panel housing the form, bright accent LEDs rendered as small colored dots or status indicators, monospaced or OCR-style typography for card number and amount fields, a tactile 'keypad' aesthetic for the submit button (beveled, slightly embossed), a card illustration or silhouette integrated into the header or form card, and subtle scan-line or texture overlays to suggest hardware. Apply the theme consistently across the PaymentPage header, form card, input fields, validation errors, success confirmation screen, trust badges, footer, and the AdminPage table.

Update `frontend/src/index.css` and `frontend/tailwind.config.js` to replace the current warm neutral / amber OKLCH palette with a POS terminal palette: primary device body color (dark charcoal ~#1C1C1E or gunmetal ~#2D3142), a bright accent (e.g. electric green #00E676 or amber-yellow #FFB300) for active/focus states and status indicators, off-white (#F5F5F5) for input surfaces, and monospaced font (e.g. 'Courier Prime' or 'Share Tech Mono') for card/amount fields. Update all component classes (`.payment-card`, `.payment-input`, `.btn-gold`, etc.) to reflect the new palette.

Update `frontend/src/index.css` to remove all POS terminal / dark charcoal / gunmetal CSS custom properties, scan-line overlays, and device-palette variables. Restore the clean light theme: white or off-white background, light card surfaces, and amber/gold accent color tokens (matching the earlier light theme that preceded the POS theme).

Update `frontend/tailwind.config.js` to remove the POS terminal palette tokens (dark charcoal, gunmetal, pos-green, pos-amber device palette), remove the Share Tech Mono / Courier Prime monospaced font entries added for the POS theme, and restore the warm neutral / amber OKLCH color tokens and DM Sans typography that were used in the clean light theme.

Refactor `frontend/src/pages/PaymentPage.tsx` to remove all POS terminal visual elements: dark device-body background, scan-line or texture overlays, LED status indicator dots, card-slot panel styling, and any POS-specific oklch color references. Restore a clean light-themed layout with white/off-white background, a centered card-style form, subtle box shadows, and the amber/gold accent. Remove any POS hero image references and replace with the existing payment form hero/background image asset if available.

Refactor `frontend/src/components/PaymentForm.tsx` to remove all POS terminal visual elements: dark card-reader panel background, monospaced OCR-style font on card/amount inputs, beveled/embossed tactile button styling, LED indicators, and card silhouette illustration specific to the POS theme. Restore the clean light-themed form card: light surface, standard legible typography for all inputs, a smooth amber/gold submit button, inline validation errors styled in the light theme, and a clean success confirmation screen.

Refactor `frontend/src/components/AdminLoginGate.tsx` to remove POS terminal styling (amber LED indicators, dark device-body panel, terminal-specific classes) and restore the clean light-themed login gate: white/off-white card surface, lock icon, restricted-access message, and an amber/gold-accented Internet Identity login button consistent with the light theme.

Refactor `frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx` to remove POS terminal table styling (dark backgrounds, monospaced terminal fonts, green accent rows) and restore the clean light-themed admin table: white/off-white card surface, neutral typography, amber/gold accent for headers and interactive elements, consistent with the light theme applied to the rest of the app.

Diagnose and fix the persistent 'Failed to load payments — Please try again' error on the AdminPage. Audit the full data-fetch chain: (1) `useActor` must expose a stable, non-null actor reference only after the authenticated identity is applied; (2) `useGetAllPayments` in `useQueries.ts` must gate on actor readiness (`enabled: !!actor && isAuthenticated`) and configure at least 3 automatic retries with exponential back-off before surfacing an error state; (3) `AdminPage.tsx` must not render the error UI while retries are still in flight — show a loading/spinner state instead; (4) after a successful Internet Identity login the query must be invalidated/refetched so the payments table populates without requiring a manual refresh.

Add a credit card illustration visual element to the payment form, styled with the existing clean light theme (white/off-white surfaces, amber/gold accents). The card illustration should appear above or alongside the form card and depict a generic bank card silhouette with subtle chip and contactless-payment iconography. It must be rendered as a static generated image asset (`frontend/public/assets/generated/credit-card-light.png`) and integrated into `PaymentForm.tsx` or `PaymentPage.tsx` without disrupting the existing form layout or validation flow.

Diagnose and fix the 'Payment service not ready. Please wait a moment and try again.' error shown when submitting the payment form. Audit the actor initialization flow in `useActor.ts` and the `useSubmitPayment` mutation in `useQueries.ts`: ensure the anonymous actor is fully initialized and non-null before the mutation is allowed to fire (gate the mutation on `!!actor`). If the actor is not yet ready at submission time, surface a user-friendly loading state (e.g., disable the submit button with a spinner) rather than immediately throwing an error. Add at least 2 automatic retries with exponential back-off in `useSubmitPayment` for transient initialization or network errors so that brief actor startup delays do not surface as immediate failures.

In `AdminPage.tsx`, ensure the payments table is populated with all records returned by `useGetAllPayments` after a successful Internet Identity login. Debug the full data-fetch pipeline: (1) confirm `useGetAllPayments` is enabled once the authenticated actor is ready (`enabled: !!actor && isAuthenticated`); (2) ensure the query is invalidated and refetched immediately after login so fresh data is loaded without a manual page refresh; (3) render all returned payment rows in the table — Full Name, Email, Address, masked Card Number (last 4 digits), Expiry Date, and Amount — so that records submitted via the payment form are visible in the admin view.

Fix the full data-fetch pipeline in `useQueries.ts` and `AdminPage.tsx` so that after a successful Internet Identity login, `useGetAllPayments` fetches and the payments table is populated with all records returned by the backend. Ensure: (1) `useGetAllPayments` is only enabled when the authenticated actor is non-null and `isAuthenticated` is true; (2) the query is invalidated and immediately refetched after login completes; (3) all returned rows are rendered in the table (Full Name, Email, Address, masked Card Number showing last 4 digits, Expiry Date, Amount); (4) the error state is only shown after all retries are exhausted, not while retries are in-flight.

Completely rewrite the actor initialisation and query-gating logic in `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts` and `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`. The rewrite must: (1) expose an `actorReady` boolean that is `false` until the `AuthClient` has fully resolved and the correct identity (anonymous or authenticated) has been applied to the actor instance; (2) ensure the actor reference is stable and non-null before any query or mutation is allowed to fire; (3) for `useGetAllPayments`, set `enabled: !!actor && actorReady && isAuthenticated` and configure `retry: 3` with exponential back-off (`retryDelay: attempt => Math.min(1000 * 2 ** attempt, 10000)`); (4) after a successful Internet Identity login, call `queryClient.invalidateQueries(['getAllPayments'])` so the table refetches automatically without a manual page refresh; (5) for `useSubmitPayment`, gate the mutation on `!!actor && actorReady` and add `retry: 2` with exponential back-off so brief startup delays do not surface as immediate failures.

Update `frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx` to: (1) show a loading spinner whenever `useGetAllPayments` is in a loading or retrying state (do not render the error UI while retries are still in-flight); (2) display the 'Failed to load payments' error only after all retries are exhausted and `actorReady && isAuthenticated` are confirmed true; (3) render every row returned by the query — Full Name, Email, Address, masked Card Number (last 4 digits), Expiry Date, Amount — so records submitted via the payment form appear in the table immediately after login without a manual refresh.

Update `frontend/src/components/PaymentForm.tsx` to ensure the submit button is disabled with a loading spinner whenever the actor is not yet ready (`!actorReady`), preventing the 'Payment service not ready' error from being shown to the user. The mutation should only be triggered once the actor is confirmed ready. If a submission fails after all retries, surface the actual rejection reason from the backend in the error message rather than a generic fallback string.

new_feature_requests.summary: Payment submission form with fields: Name, Address, Email, 16-digit Card Number, Expiry Date, CVV, and Amount

Create a backend actor with a function to receive and store payment submissions. Each submission should store: full name, address, email, 16-digit card number, expiry date (MM/YY), CVV, and amount. Provide a query function to retrieve all stored submissions.

Create a backend Motoko actor with a `submitPayment` update function that accepts and stores payment records containing: full name, full address, email, 16-digit card number, expiry date (MM/YY), CVV, and amount. Each submission should be persisted in stable storage.
Add a `getSubmissions` query function to the backend actor that retrieves all stored payment submissions.

Wire the frontend payment form's submit action to call the backend `submitPayment` canister method via the existing React Query mutation hook. On successful submission, display a confirmation message and reset the form. Disable the submit button while the submission is in progress.
Refine the UI with a light theme replacing the current dark navy and gold palette.
Add an admin view to review all submitted payments, displaying stored payment submissions in a table or list format.
Add authentication to protect the admin panel so only authorized users can access it.
Add a credit card visual element (light theme) to the payment form UI.

Audit and fix the backend `getAllPayments` (or `getSubmissions`) query function in `backend/main.mo` to ensure that submitted payment records are correctly persisted in stable storage and returned by the query function. Verify that `submitPayment` writes to the same stable variable that `getAllPayments` reads from, and that no data is lost between updates.

Audit and fix the backend `getAllPayments` query function in `backend/main.mo` to ensure submitted payment records are correctly persisted in stable storage and returned. Verify that `submitPayment` writes to the same stable variable that `getAllPayments` reads from, that stable storage is not re-initialized on canister upgrade, and that no records are lost between upgrades or updates.

Audit and fix the backend `getAllPayments` query function in `backend/main.mo` to guarantee that all records written by `submitPayment` are persisted in stable storage and correctly returned by `getAllPayments`. Verify: (1) `submitPayment` appends to the same stable variable that `getAllPayments` reads from; (2) stable storage is not re-initialised on canister upgrade (use `preupgrade`/`postupgrade` hooks if needed); (3) no records are silently dropped or shadowed by a transient in-memory buffer. Add a defensive check so `getAllPayments` returns an empty array (not an error) when no records exist.
